danger_signals.visual_form: Score display increment and opposing team's progress bar
Quarter timer countdown, visual urgency increases
Ball control indicator shift, player highlight change
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of agency and scoring opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Own team player highlighted with ball control
Higher score display, progress bar advantage
Own team players positioned between opponent and basket

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Los Angeles Lakers association (Pat Riley connection)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team roster displays distinct uniform colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Court side signage and team branding visible in arena

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single game setup with scoreboard and quarter display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard play without tournament progression stakes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament bracket/progression display, cumulative standing indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multi-game series with advancement requirements and elimination consequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Quarter indicator display (1/4), full energy level indicators for players
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum player performance capacity, fresh positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Quarter indicator display (4/4), potential fatigue visual indicators on player movement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced player stamina, strategic substitution necessity becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display proximity, visual parity in progress bars
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High tension gameplay, aggressive play calling increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dominant score display, substantial progress bar advantage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Conservative play patterns, potential tempo changes

environmental_storytelling.example: Team-specific court presentations and sideline visual elements reinforce team faction identity across all eight distinct teams
Each of eight teams displays visually distinct uniform colors and patterns to enable rapid player allegiance identification during play

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: Quarter-by-quarter score accumulation tells the unfolding story of game momentum and performance trajectory

environmental_storytelling.example: Defensive vs. offensive positioning changes communicate strategic world-state shifts (aggressive vs. conservative play modes)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Highlighted/emphasized visual state, responsive to input commands, ball control visualization
Standard positioning without emphasis, movement follows strategic formation
Highlighted to show threat status, active movement toward scoring position
Positioned between player and basket, movement reactive to possession changes
Full visual animation capability, rapid response to commands
Reduced animation speed or visual slumping indicators, slower positioning response

notes: Pat Riley Basketball is a traditional basketball simulator with limited documentation of visual manifestation systems. Data extracted focuses on inferred visual communication methods typical of early 1990s sports games on Genesis. Eight distinct teams (regional U.S. teams plus international teams: Japan, Brazil, China, Italy, Mexico, England, Korea) provide faction-level differentiation through roster and uniform design. World-state communication relies primarily on: score displays, quarter/time displays, possession indicators, and player positioning. The game's paradigm emphasizes regulated sport mechanics over narrative worldbuilding, limiting complex environmental storytelling techniques. Danger and safety signals center on gameplay status (scoring threats, possession loss, time pressure) rather than narrative danger. The Pat Riley naming and Lakers association provide contextual world-state (real sports reference, early 1990s NBA context) but minimal in-game visual manifestation of this reference.
